title: Traffic-gap and pit-window feasibility features
summary: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize that pit timing depends not just on tyre age but on undercut range, out-lap warmup, and whether the car can rejoin into clean air after the pit-loss delta.
rationale: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize that pit timing depends not just on tyre age but on undercut range, out-lap warmup, and whether the car can rejoin into clean air after the pit-loss delta. Your current best features model tyre finish windows, but not rejoin-window feasibility against surrounding traffic.
implementation_hint: Within each race-lap, estimate local field density and projected rejoin slot using lap position, nearby rivals' lap pace, and a race-specific pit-loss proxy inferred from historical `PitStop==1` laps. Add features like projected positions lost if pitting now, clean-air flag after stop, undercut-cover margin, and traffic-blocked stop window.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination around borderline stops where tyre wear says 'pit soon' but traffic makes 'this lap' suboptimal.
risk: Pit-loss proxies are noisy without explicit gap times; overly aggressive approximations can add variance.
